nodes.question: Does the engineering team exceed 40 developers, indicating a mature or enterprise environment?
Is the team completely new or under 10 developers?
Does the team have a dedicated Platform Team (Platform Maturity)?

nodes.rationale: For startups under 10 developers, heavy onboarding dashboards are overkill. Focus purely on the 'Golden Rule' (setup < 1 hour via Docker/DevContainers), a visible 30-day roadmap, and pairing with a Senior for immediate TTFC (< 3 days).

For teams between 10-40 developers, tribal knowledge becomes a severe bottleneck. Implement a strict 4-week structured plan (Context, Deep Dive, Guided Autonomy, Simulated Seniority) with a formalized Buddy System (2 sessions/week).

With >40 devs but no formal Platform team, onboarding must be heavily documented to survive scale. Mandate up-to-date ADRs, centralized logging walkthroughs, and strict Quality Gates to block low-quality PRs from overwhelmed newcomers.

For mature platform organizations, onboarding is a measurable product. Implement automated TTFC/TTFP tracking, Friction Surveys, and Heatmaps of PR rejection rates to continuously refactor the onboarding docs and internal developer portals.
